from pyspark.sql import functions as F

CASE_REF_FILE = f"{mpesc_base_path}/MPESC-EXTRACT/Horizon_CaseReference.csv"
LLATTR_CASE_GLOB = f"{mpesc_base_path}/TRANSFORM-CASE/LLAttr/Case_*/part*.csv"

case_ref = (
    spark.read
        .format("csv")
        .option("header", True)
        .option("sep", "|")
        .option("multiLine", True)          # 🔥 CRITICAL FIX
        .option("quote", '"')               # handle quoted fields
        .option("escape", '"')              # handle escaped quotes
        .option("mode", "PERMISSIVE")       # don’t fail on bad rows
        .option("inferSchema", False)       # safer for messy exports
        .load(CASE_REF_FILE)
)

display(case_ref.limit(10))
print("case_ref rows:", case_ref.count())

PREFIXES = ["ROW/"]

prefix_filter = None
for p in PREFIXES:
    cond = F.col("CaseReference").startswith(p)
    prefix_filter = cond if prefix_filter is None else (prefix_filter | cond)

filtered_cases = (
    case_ref
    .filter(prefix_filter)
    .select(
        F.col("CaseUniqueId").alias("caseReference"),
        F.col("CaseReference").alias("hznReference"),
        F.col("CaseTypeId").alias("caseTypeId"),
        F.col("CaseNodeId").alias("caseNodeId"),
        F.col("CaseCreateDate").alias("caseCreateDate"),
        F.col("CaseCloseDate").alias("caseCloseDate"),
        F.col("CaseCloseStatus").alias("caseCloseStatus"),
        F.col("CaseOwnerId").alias("caseOwnerId")
    )
)

display(filtered_cases.limit(10))
print("filtered cases:", filtered_cases.count())

LLATTR_CASE_GLOB = f"{mpesc_base_path}/MPESC-EXTRACT/LLAttr/Case_95498/part*.csv"

ll_case = (
    spark.read
        .format("csv")
        .option("header", True)
        .option("sep", "|")
        .option("multiLine", True)         
        .option("quote", '"')
        .option("escape", '"')
        .option("mode", "PERMISSIVE")
        .option("columnNameOfCorruptRecord", "_corrupt_record")
        .option("inferSchema", False)      
        .load(LLATTR_CASE_GLOB)
)


cases_keys = filtered_cases.select(F.col("caseNodeId").cast("string").alias("caseNodeIdKey")).dropDuplicates()

attrs_filtered = (
    ll_case
    .withColumn("ID", F.col("ID").cast("string"))
    .join(cases_keys, F.col("ID") == F.col("caseNodeIdKey"), "inner")
    .drop("caseNodeIdKey")
)

print("attrs_filtered:", attrs_filtered.count())
display(attrs_filtered.limit(5))

drop_cols = [c for c in ["AttrID", "AttrType", "DataType",  "RowNum", "Partition", "FileName"] if c in attrs_filtered.columns]

attrs_clean = attrs_filtered.drop(*drop_cols)

print("Dropped cols:", drop_cols)
print("Remaining attr cols:", len(attrs_clean.columns))

attrs_one_row = attrs_clean.dropDuplicates(["ID"])

cases_joined = (
    filtered_cases.alias("c")
    .join(
        attrs_one_row.alias("a"),
        F.col("c.caseNodeId").cast("string") == F.col("a.ID").cast("string"),
        "left"
    )
    .drop("ID")  
)

display(cases_joined.limit(10))
print("cases_joined rows:", cases_joined.count())

TYPE_OF_CASE_FILE = f"{mpesc_base_path}/MPESC-EXTRACT/Horizon_TypeOfCase.csv"

type_of_case = (
    spark.read
        .option("header", True)
        .option("sep", "|")
        .option("inferSchema", True)
        .csv(TYPE_OF_CASE_FILE)
)

final_df = (
    cases_joined.alias("x")
    .join(type_of_case.alias("t"), F.col("x.caseTypeId") == F.col("t.ID"), "left")
    .drop(F.col("t.ID"))
)

display(final_df.limit(10))

attr_rename_map = {
    "89": "act",
    "100": "appealAgainst",
    "53": "appellantFormTypeSubmitted",
    "84": "appellantReasonsWRNotSufficient",
    "91": "applicationSubtype",
    "80": "appointedBy",
    "104": "band",
    "40": "bespokeIndicator",
    "85": "caseProcess",
    "52": "casePublishFlag",
    "2": "caseTypeAttr",
    "81": "caseworkMarker",
    "47": "caseworkReason",
    "45": "costsAppliedForIndicator",
    "95": "deccRef",
    "74": "decision",
    "79": "decisionBranch",
    "92": "decisionClearForDespatch",
    "46": "decisionIssue",
    "9": "developmentType",
    "17": "developmentAllegationDescription",
    "73": "englandWalesIndicator",
    "99": "eprType",
    "83": "fileLocation",
    "75": "groundADPALapsedIndicator",
    "82": "importantInformation",
    "7": "jurisdiction",
    "54": "level",
    "51": "linkedStatus",
    "76": "lpaCode",
    "77": "lpaName",
    "70": "nationalPlanningCaseworkReference",
    "94": "nfa",
    "93": "omaStance",
    "69": "otherRelatedAppeals",
    "72": "portalUrl",
    "5": "procedure",
    "4": "procedureAppellant",
    "3": "procedureLpa",
    "55": "processingStateAttr",
    "68": "redeterminedIndicator",
    "87": "regulation",
    "88": "regulator",
    "96": "sch6Notice",
    "90": "section",
    "97": "slaDateMet",
    "71": "sourceIndicator",
    "78": "specialCircumstances",
    "60": "specialism",
    "102": "standardPriority",
    "98": "targetDateMet",
    "101": "validity"
}

print(sorted(final_df_named.columns))
display(final_df_named)
df = final_df_named.cache()
df.count()

case_type_map = {
    "2":  "call-ins",
    "17": "coastal-access",
    "18": "common-land",
    "19": "environment",
    "20": "high-hedges",
    "21": "other-sos-casework",
    "22": "purchase-notices",
    "23": "rights-of-way"
}

keys_list = list(case_type_map.keys())
map_expr = F.create_map([F.lit(x) for kv in case_type_map.items() for x in kv])

df_mapped = df.withColumn(
    "caseTypeName",
    F.when(
        F.col("caseTypeId").cast("string").isin(keys_list),
        map_expr.getItem(F.col("caseTypeId").cast("string"))
    ).otherwise(F.lit(None))
)

df_mapped.select("caseTypeId", "caseTypeName") \
    .distinct() \
    .orderBy(F.col("caseTypeId").cast("int")) \
    .show(truncate=False)
display(df_mapped.limit(10))
df.groupBy("applicationSubtype").count().show(100, truncate=False)

application_subtype_map = {
    # Rights of Way
    "DMMO under WCA: 7": "opposed-dmmo",
    "DMMO under WCA: 8": "opposed-dmmo",
    "Appeal under WCA: 14A": "schedule-14-appeal",
    "Direction under WCA: 14D": "schedule-14-direction",
    "Diversion under HA: 4": "opposed-ppo-ha80",
    "Creation under HA: 6": "opposed-ppo-ha80",
    "Extinguishment under HA: 3": "opposed-ppo-ha80",
    "Stopping up or diversion of highways": "opposed-ppo-ha80",
    "Stopping up or diversion under TCPA: 5": "opposed-ppo-tcpa90",
    "Acquisition of land in connection with highways":"opposed-ppo-ha80",

    # Trees / High Hedges
    "High Hedges": "high-hedges",
    "Tree Preservation Orders": "tree-lopping",
    "Tree Preservation Orders (Fast track)": "tree-lopping",
    "Tree Replacement Notices": "tree-lopping",
    "Tree Replacement Notices (Fast track)": "tree-lopping",

    # Common Land
    "Compulsory Purchase Order": "compulsory-purchase-cl",

    "Notice Appeals": "notice-appeal",

    # Other SOS
    "Discontinuance Order": "discontinuance-notice",
}

map_expr = F.create_map(
    [F.lit(x) for kv in application_subtype_map.items() for x in kv]
)

df_mapped = df_mapped.withColumn(
    "applicationSubtypeMapped",
    map_expr.getItem(F.col("applicationSubtype"))
)

df_mapped.select(
    "applicationSubtype",
    "applicationSubtypeMapped"
).distinct().orderBy("applicationSubtype").show(truncate=False)

from notebookutils import mssparkutils
from pyspark.sql import functions as F

workspace = mssparkutils.env.getWorkspaceName()

# -------------------------------------------------
# Environment mapping
# -------------------------------------------------

if workspace == "pins-synw-odw-prod-uks":
    server = "pins-sql-peas-primary-training.database.windows.net"
    database = "pins-sqldb-peas-training"
    akv_name = "pins-kv-peas-training"
    secret_name = "peas-sql-app-connection-string"

elif workspace == "pins-synw-odw-dev-uks":
    server = "pins-sql-peas-primary-dev.database.windows.net"
    database = "pins-sqldb-peas-dev"
    akv_name = "pinskvsynwodwdevuks"
    secret_name = "mpesc-sql-password"

else:
    raise ValueError(f"Unknown workspace '{workspace}'")

# -------------------------------------------------
# Build JDBC URL
# -------------------------------------------------

url = (
    f"jdbc:sqlserver://{server}:1433;"
    f"database={database};"
    "encrypt=true;"
    "trustServerCertificate=false;"
    "hostNameInCertificate=*.database.windows.net;"
    "loginTimeout=30;"
)

# -------------------------------------------------
# Get secret
# -------------------------------------------------

secret_value = mssparkutils.credentials.getSecret(
    akv_name,
    secret_name
)

# -------------------------------------------------
# Training uses connection string secret
# Dev uses direct password secret
# -------------------------------------------------

if secret_name == "peas-sql-app-connection-string":
    parts = {
        kv.split("=", 1)[0].strip().lower(): kv.split("=", 1)[1].strip()
        for kv in secret_value.split(";")
        if "=" in kv
    }

    user = parts.get("user id") or parts.get("uid") or parts.get("user")
    password = parts.get("password") or parts.get("pwd")

    if not user:
        raise ValueError(f"User ID not found in {secret_name} (KV={akv_name})")

    if not password:
        raise ValueError(f"Password not found in {secret_name} (KV={akv_name})")

else:
    # Dev
    user = "peas_app_ZXzkE0vP"
    password = secret_value

# -------------------------------------------------
# Read dbo.[User]
# -------------------------------------------------

user_df = (
    spark.read
    .format("jdbc")
    .option("url", url)
    .option("dbtable", "dbo.[User]")
    .option("user", user)
    .option("password", password)
    .load()
    .select("legacyId", "idpUserId")
)

# -------------------------------------------------
# Clean mapping
# -------------------------------------------------

user_clean = (
    user_df
    .filter(
        (F.col("legacyId").isNotNull()) &
        (F.col("legacyId") != "undefined") &
        (F.col("idpUserId").isNotNull()) &
        (F.col("idpUserId") != "undefined")
    )
    .select(
        F.col("legacyId").cast("string").alias("legacyId_str"),
        F.col("idpUserId")
    )
)

display(user_clean.limit(20))
print("User rows:", user_clean.count())

from pyspark.sql import functions as F

# Clean user table once
user_clean = (
    user_df
    .filter(
        (F.col("legacyId").isNotNull()) &
        (F.col("legacyId") != "undefined") &
        (F.col("idpUserId").isNotNull()) &
        (F.col("idpUserId") != "undefined")
    )
    .select(
        F.col("legacyId").cast("string").alias("caseOwnerId_str"),
        F.col("idpUserId")
    )
)

df_mapped = (
    df_mapped
    .withColumn("caseOwnerId_str", F.col("caseOwnerId").cast("string"))
    .join(user_clean, on="caseOwnerId_str", how="left")
    .withColumn(
        "caseOfficerId",
        F.col("idpUserId")   # ✅ stays null if not matched
    )
    .drop("caseOwnerId_str", "idpUserId")

df_mapped = df_mapped.withColumn(
    "receivedDate",
    F.coalesce(F.col("caseCreateDate"), F.current_timestamp())
)

df_mapped = df_mapped.withColumn("name", F.col("hznReference"))
df_mapped.select("caseOfficerId").show(10, truncate=False)

from notebookutils import mssparkutils
from pyspark.sql import functions as F

workspace = mssparkutils.env.getWorkspaceName()

if workspace == "pins-synw-odw-prod-uks":
    storage_account_name = "pinsstmpescproduksqz8bvk"
    server = "pins-sql-peas-primary-training.database.windows.net"
    database = "pins-sqldb-peas-training"
    akv_name = "pins-kv-peas-training"
    secret_name = "peas-sql-app-connection-string"

elif workspace == "pins-synw-odw-dev-uks":
    storage_account_name = "pinsstmpescdevuksw49hqz"
    server = "pins-sql-peas-primary-dev.database.windows.net"
    database = "pins-sqldb-peas-dev"
    akv_name = "pinskvsynwodwdevuks"
    secret_name = "mpesc-sql-password"

else:
    raise ValueError(f"Unknown workspace '{workspace}'")

# Build JDBC URL
url = (
    f"jdbc:sqlserver://{server}:1433;"
    f"database={database};"
    "encrypt=true;"
    "trustServerCertificate=false;"
    "hostNameInCertificate=*.database.windows.net;"
    "loginTimeout=30;"
)

# Get secret value
secret_value = mssparkutils.credentials.getSecret(akv_name, secret_name)

# Prod/training: parse user+password from connection string secret
if secret_name == "peas-sql-app-connection-string":
    parts = {
        kv.split("=", 1)[0].strip().lower(): kv.split("=", 1)[1].strip()
        for kv in secret_value.split(";")
        if "=" in kv
    }
    sql_user = parts.get("user id") or parts.get("uid") or parts.get("user")
    sql_password = parts.get("password") or parts.get("pwd")

    if not sql_user:
        raise ValueError(f"User ID not found in {secret_name} (KV={akv_name})")
    if not sql_password:
        raise ValueError(f"Password not found in {secret_name} (KV={akv_name})")

# Dev: fixed user + password secret
else:
    sql_user = "peas_app_ZXzkE0vP"
    sql_password = secret_value

# Pull Address GUIDs using legacyCaseId
address_lookup = (
    spark.read.format("jdbc")
    .option("url", url)
    .option("query", """
        SELECT
            CAST(legacyCaseId AS varchar(50)) AS legacyCaseId,
            CAST(id AS varchar(36)) AS addressGuid
        FROM dbo.Address
        WHERE legacyCaseId IS NOT NULL
    """)
    .option("user", sql_user)
    .option("password", sql_password)
    .load()
    .dropDuplicates(["legacyCaseId"])
)

display(address_lookup.limit(20))
print("address_lookup rows:", address_lookup.count())

df_mapped = (
    df_mapped
    .withColumn("caseNodeId_str", F.col("caseNodeId").cast("string"))
    .join(
        F.broadcast(address_lookup)
            .withColumnRenamed("legacyCaseId", "caseNodeId_str"),
        on="caseNodeId_str",
        how="left"
    )
    .drop("caseNodeId_str")
)

# 3) Quick check
df_mapped.select("caseNodeId", "addressGuid").show(20, truncate=False)

df_mapped = (
    df_mapped
    .withColumn(
        "band_id",
        F.when(F.col("band") == "Band 1", "band-1")
         .when(F.col("band") == "Band 2", "band-2")
         .when(F.col("band") == "Band 3", "band-3")
         .otherwise(None)
    )
)

display(df_mapped.select("band", "band_id").distinct())

df_mapped = (
    df_mapped
    .withColumn(
        "processingStateId",
        F.when(F.col("processingStateAttr") == "Decision Issued", "decision-recommendation-issued")
         .when(F.col("processingStateAttr") == "New Case", "new-case")
         .when(F.col("processingStateAttr") == "Validated", "validated")
         .when(F.col("processingStateAttr") == "Case In Progress", "in-progress")
         .when(F.col("processingStateAttr") == "Turned Away", "rejected")
         .when(F.col("processingStateAttr") == "Closed - Opened in Error", "closed-opened-in-error")
         .when(F.col("processingStateAttr") == "Ready for Inspector Action/Awaiting Event", "ready-for-inspector")
         .when(F.col("processingStateAttr") == "File Sent to Chart for Inspector", "allocate-inspector")
         .when(F.col("processingStateAttr") == "Appeal Withdrawn", "withdrawn")
         .when(F.col("processingStateAttr") == "Order withdrawn", "withdrawn")
         .when(F.col("processingStateAttr") == "Incomplete", "invalid")
         .when(F.col("processingStateAttr") == "Invalid - Missing Information", "invalid")
         .when(F.col("processingStateAttr") == "Invalid - No Right of Appeal", "invalid")
         .when(F.col("processingStateAttr") == "Abeyance", "in-abeyance")
         .when(F.col("processingStateAttr") == "Event", "event")
    )
)

display(
    df_mapped
    .select("processingStateAttr", "processingStateId")
    .distinct()
    .orderBy("processingStateAttr")

df_mapped = df_mapped.withColumn(
    "priority",
    F.when(F.trim(F.col("standardPriority")) == "Priority 1", F.lit("high"))
     .when(F.trim(F.col("standardPriority")) == "Priority 2", F.lit("medium"))
     .when(F.trim(F.col("standardPriority")) == "Priority 3", F.lit("low"))
     .when(F.lower(F.trim(F.col("standardPriority"))) == "undefined", F.lit(None))  # or null
     .otherwise(F.lit(None))

from pyspark.sql.functions import col, trim, when, expr

cases_path = f"{mpesc_base_path}/config/Cases/Cases.csv"

cases_df = (
    spark.read.format("csv")
    .option("header", "true")
    .option("inferSchema", "false")
    .option("sep", ",")          # change to ";" or "\t" if needed
    .option("quote", "\"")
    .option("escape", "\"")
    .option("multiLine", "true")
    .load(cases_path)
)

print("Columns:", cases_df.columns)
display(cases_df)

from pyspark.sql.functions import trim, col

case_ids = [
    str(r["Case ID"]).strip()
    for r in cases_df
        .select(trim(col("Case ID")).alias("Case ID"))
        .collect()
    if r["Case ID"] is not None and str(r["Case ID"]).strip() != ""
]

case_ids

from pyspark.sql import functions as F


cases_df2 = cases_df


if "GUID" not in cases_df2.columns:
    cases_df2 = cases_df2.withColumn("GUID", F.lit(None).cast("string"))


cases_df2 = (
    cases_df2
    .withColumn("Case ID", F.trim(F.col("Case ID")).cast("string"))
    .withColumn("GUID", F.trim(F.col("GUID")).cast("string"))
    .filter(F.col("Case ID").isNotNull() & (F.col("Case ID") != ""))
)


cases_df2 = cases_df2.withColumn(
    "GUID",
    F.when(F.col("GUID").isNull() | (F.col("GUID") == ""), F.expr("uuid()"))
     .otherwise(F.col("GUID"))
)

display(cases_df2)


tmp_folder = f"{mpesc_base_path}/config/Cases/_tmp_cases_with_guids/"
final_folder = f"{mpesc_base_path}/config/Cases/"
final_file = final_folder + "Cases.csv"


(cases_df2
 .select("Case ID", "GUID")
 .coalesce(1)
 .write.mode("overwrite")
 .format("csv")
 .option("header", "true")
 .save(tmp_folder)
)


files = mssparkutils.fs.ls(tmp_folder)
part_files = [f.path for f in files if f.name.startswith("part-") and f.name.endswith(".csv")]
if len(part_files) != 1:
    raise Exception(f"Expected exactly 1 part file in temp folder, found {len(part_files)}: {part_files}")

part_file = part_files[0]


try:
    mssparkutils.fs.rm(final_file, True)
except:
    pass

mssparkutils.fs.mv(part_file, final_file)


mssparkutils.fs.rm(tmp_folder, True)

print("Updated Cases.csv with GUIDs (without overwriting existing GUIDs):", final_file)

from pyspark.sql import functions as F

# 1) Normalise Cases.csv keys + GUIDs
case_guid_map = (
    cases_df2
    .select(
        F.trim(F.col("Case ID").cast("string")).alias("caseNodeId_str"),
        F.trim(F.col("GUID").cast("string")).alias("caseGuid"),
    )
    # treat empty string GUID as null
    .withColumn("caseGuid", F.when(F.col("caseGuid") == "", None).otherwise(F.col("caseGuid")))
    # keep only rows that can actually map
    .filter(F.col("caseNodeId_str").isNotNull())
    .dropDuplicates(["caseNodeId_str"])
)

# 2) Normalise df_mapped join key
df_mapped2 = (
    df_mapped
    .withColumn("caseNodeId_str", F.trim(F.col("caseNodeId").cast("string")))
    .join(case_guid_map, on="caseNodeId_str", how="left")
)

# 3) DEBUG: prove whether they’re missing from Cases.csv or just not joining
missing_from_cases_csv = (
    df_mapped2
    .filter(F.col("caseGuid").isNull())
    .select("caseNodeId_str")
    .distinct()
)

display(missing_from_cases_csv)

print("df_mapped rows:", df_mapped.count())
print("mapped rows:", df_mapped2.filter(F.col("caseGuid").isNotNull()).count())
print("missing rows:", df_mapped2.filter(F.col("caseGuid").isNull()).count())

# 4) If you truly want to DROP unmapped rows:
df_mapped = df_mapped2.filter(F.col("caseGuid").isNotNull())

mod_path = f"{mpesc_base_path}/MPESC-EXTRACT/LLAttr/Modification_15809003/"

mods_raw = (
    spark.read.format("csv")
    .option("header", "true")
    .option("sep", "|")          
    .option("multiLine", "true")
    .option("escape", "\"")
    .option("quote", "\"")
    .option("inferSchema", "true")
    .load(mod_path)
)

print("mods_raw columns:", mods_raw.columns)
display(mods_raw.limit(5))



mods_cols = mods_raw.columns
col1 = mods_cols[0]   # column 1
col3 = mods_cols[2]   # column 3

mods_pick = (
    mods_raw
    .select(
        F.col(col1).cast("string").alias("mod_id"),
        F.col(col3).cast("string").alias("mod_attribute")
    )
    .dropDuplicates(["mod_id"])
)

display(mods_pick.limit(10))

df_col1 = df_mapped.columns[0]  # column 1 of df_mapped by position

df_mapped = (
    df_mapped
    .withColumn("df_join_id", F.col(df_col1).cast("string"))
    .join(mods_pick, F.col("df_join_id") == F.col("mod_id"), "left")
    .drop("df_join_id", "mod_id")
    .withColumnRenamed("mod_attribute", "modification_attribute_col3")
)


print("Rows in df_mapped:", df_mapped.count())

display(
    df_mapped
    .filter(F.col("modification_attribute_col3").isNull())
    .select(df_col1)
    .distinct()
    .limit(50)
)

display(
    df_mapped
    .filter(F.col("modification_attribute_col3").isNotNull())
    .select(df_col1, "modification_attribute_col3", "caseGuid")
    .limit(50)

display(df_mapped)

df_mapped = (
    df_mapped
    .withColumn(
        "advertisedModificationId",
        F.when(
            F.col("modification_attribute_col3").cast("int").between(1, 5),
            F.concat(
                F.lit("advertised-modification-"),
                F.col("modification_attribute_col3").cast("int").cast("string")
            )
        )
    )
)

display(
    df_mapped.select(
        "modification_attribute_col3",
        "advertisedModificationId"
    ).distinct()
)

display(df_mapped)

from pyspark.sql import functions as F

# --------------------------------------------
# Load Case Dates (Receipt Date) and join onto df_mapped
# --------------------------------------------

case_dates_path = f"{mpesc_base_path}/MPESC-EXTRACT/LLAttr/Case_Dates_95499/*.csv"

case_dates_raw = (
    spark.read
        .format("csv")
        .option("header", "true")
        .option("sep", "|")
        .option("quote", "\"")
        .option("escape", "\"")
        .option("multiLine", "false")
        .load(case_dates_path)
)

# Receipt Date is AttrID 2.
# In these LLAttr extracts the column is usually literally named "2" (string),
# but this also safely handles if it's named "Receipt Date".
receipt_col = (
    F.col("2") if "2" in case_dates_raw.columns
    else F.col("Receipt Date") if "Receipt Date" in case_dates_raw.columns
    else None
)

if receipt_col is None:
    raise ValueError(f"Couldn't find Receipt Date column. Columns are: {case_dates_raw.columns}")

case_dates = (
    case_dates_raw
    .select(
        F.trim(F.col("ID").cast("string")).alias("legacyCaseId"),
        F.to_timestamp(receipt_col).alias("caseReceiptDate")
    )
    .dropDuplicates(["legacyCaseId"])
)

print("Case_Dates rows:", case_dates.count())
print("Null receipt dates:", case_dates.filter(F.col("caseReceiptDate").isNull()).count())
display(case_dates.orderBy(F.col("caseReceiptDate").desc_nulls_last()).limit(20))

df_mapped = df_mapped.withColumn(
    "caseNodeId_str",
    F.trim(F.col("caseNodeId").cast("string"))
)

# ---- make a join key on case_dates (legacyCaseId -> string) ----
case_dates = case_dates.withColumn(
    "legacyCaseId",
    F.trim(F.col("legacyCaseId").cast("string"))
).withColumnRenamed("legacyCaseId", "caseNodeId_str")

# ---- join ----
df_mapped = (
    df_mapped
    .join(case_dates, on="caseNodeId_str", how="left")
)

print("Rows after join:", df_mapped.count())
display(df_mapped.select("caseNodeId_str", "caseReceiptDate", "advertisedModificationId").limit(50))

from pyspark.sql import functions as F
from pyspark.sql.window import Window
from notebookutils import mssparkutils

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# CONFIG: where to persist the running case references
# ------------------------------------------------------------
CASE_REF_DIR = f"{mpesc_base_path}/config/CaseRef"
CASE_REF_OUT = f"{CASE_REF_DIR}/CaseRef.csv"   # Spark will create a folder; we'll write + keep it stable

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Identify the "type" column and filter ROW cases
# ------------------------------------------------------------
TYPE_COL = "caseworkType" if "caseworkType" in df_mapped.columns else ("caseTypeAttr" if "caseTypeAttr" in df_mapped.columns else None)
if TYPE_COL is None:
    raise ValueError(f"Couldn't find a type column. df_mapped has: {df_mapped.columns}")

row_cases = (
    df_mapped
    .select(
        F.col("caseNodeId_str"),
        F.col("caseCreateDate").cast("timestamp").alias("caseCreateDate"),
        F.col(TYPE_COL).cast("string").alias("caseTypeText"),
    )
    .withColumn("caseTypeText_norm", F.lower(F.trim(F.col("caseTypeText"))))
    .filter(F.col("caseNodeId_str").isNotNull())
    .filter(F.col("caseCreateDate").isNotNull())
    .filter(F.col("caseTypeText_norm").contains("rights-of-way") | F.col("caseTypeText_norm").contains("rights of way") | F.col("caseTypeText_norm").contains("row"))
    .withColumn("refPrefix", F.lit("ROW"))
    .withColumn("refYear", F.year(F.col("caseCreateDate")).cast("int"))
    .drop("caseTypeText_norm")
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Load existing CaseRef mapping if it exists (else empty)
#    and compute current max sequence per year
# ------------------------------------------------------------
def path_exists(path: str) -> bool:
    try:
        mssparkutils.fs.ls(path)
        return True
    except Exception:
        return False

if path_exists(CASE_REF_DIR):
    try:
        existing_refs = (
            spark.read.format("csv")
                .option("header", "true")
                .load(CASE_REF_DIR)  # read whole folder
        )
    except Exception:
        existing_refs = spark.createDataFrame([], "caseNodeId_str string, caseReferenceNew string, refPrefix string, refYear int, refSeq int")
else:
    existing_refs = spark.createDataFrame([], "caseNodeId_str string, caseReferenceNew string, refPrefix string, refYear int, refSeq int")

# If existing file has just caseReferenceNew, parse year/seq from it
if "refYear" not in existing_refs.columns or "refSeq" not in existing_refs.columns:
    existing_refs = (
        existing_refs
        .withColumn("caseReferenceNew", F.col("caseReferenceNew").cast("string"))
        .withColumn("refPrefix", F.split(F.col("caseReferenceNew"), "/").getItem(0))
        .withColumn("refYear", F.split(F.col("caseReferenceNew"), "/").getItem(1).cast("int"))
        .withColumn("refSeq", F.split(F.col("caseReferenceNew"), "/").getItem(2).cast("int"))
    )

existing_max_by_year = (
    existing_refs
    .filter(F.col("refPrefix") == "ROW")
    .groupBy("refYear")
    .agg(F.max("refSeq").alias("maxSeq"))
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Only generate refs for cases not already in config
#    and allocate sequences starting at 1001 (or max+1)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
row_cases_new = row_cases.join(
    existing_refs.select("caseNodeId_str").dropDuplicates(),
    on="caseNodeId_str",
    how="left_anti"
)

w = Window.partitionBy("refYear").orderBy(F.col("caseCreateDate").asc(), F.col("caseNodeId_str").asc())

row_cases_new = (
    row_cases_new
    .withColumn("rn", F.row_number().over(w))
    .join(existing_max_by_year, on="refYear", how="left")
    .withColumn("startBase", F.when(F.col("maxSeq").isNull(), F.lit(1000)).otherwise(F.col("maxSeq")))
    .withColumn("refSeq", (F.col("startBase") + F.col("rn")).cast("int"))
    .withColumn("caseReferenceNew", F.concat_ws("/", F.col("refPrefix"), F.col("refYear").cast("string"), F.col("refSeq").cast("string")))
    .drop("rn", "maxSeq", "startBase")
)

# Combine existing + new (so we keep a full mapping)
case_ref_all = (
    existing_refs.select("caseNodeId_str", "caseReferenceNew", "refPrefix", "refYear", "refSeq")
    .unionByName(row_cases_new.select("caseNodeId_str", "caseReferenceNew", "refPrefix", "refYear", "refSeq"), allowMissingColumns=True)
    .dropDuplicates(["caseNodeId_str"])
)

print("ROW cases needing new refs:", row_cases_new.count())
display(row_cases_new.orderBy("refYear", "refSeq").limit(50))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Write back to config (overwrite the folder each run)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
mssparkutils.fs.mkdirs(CASE_REF_DIR)

(
    case_ref_all
    .orderBy("refYear", "refSeq")
    .coalesce(1)
    .write
    .mode("overwrite")
    .option("header", "true")
    .csv(CASE_REF_DIR)
)

print("CaseRef mapping written to:", CASE_REF_DIR)

# ✅ ADD THIS RIGHT HERE (after overwrite, before join)
spark.catalog.clearCache()

case_ref_all = (
    spark.read
        .format("csv")
        .option("header", True)
        .load(CASE_REF_DIR)
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) (Optional) join back onto df_mapped for immediate use
# ------------------------------------------------------------
df_mapped = df_mapped.join(
    case_ref_all.select("caseNodeId_str", "caseReferenceNew"),
    on="caseNodeId_str",
    how="left"
)

display(
    df_mapped
        .select("caseNodeId_str", "caseCreateDate", TYPE_COL, "caseReferenceNew")
        .orderBy("caseCreateDate")
        .limit(50)

def norm_col(c):
    """Lower/trim + collapse whitespace; keep letters/numbers and basic separators."""
    return F.trim(F.regexp_replace(F.lower(F.col(c).cast("string")), r"\s+", " "))

# Normalised source columns
df_mapped = (
    df_mapped
    .withColumn("act_norm", norm_col("act"))
    .withColumn("section_norm", norm_col("section"))
)

# ---- ACT mapping (string -> ACT_ID) ----
# Add/extend here as needed (names must match Horizon "Name" values after normalisation)
ACT_MAP = {
    "acquisition of land act 1981": "acquisition-of-land-1981",
    "commons act 2006": "commons-2006",
    "greater london parks & open spaces order 1967": "greater-london-parks-1967",
    "highways act 1980": "highways-1980",
    "marine and coastal access act 2009": "marine-and-coastal-access-2009",
    "national trust act 1971": "national-trust-1971",
    "the coastal access reports (consideration and modification procedure)(england) regulations 2010": "coastal-access-reports-2010",
    "the national parks and access to the countryside act 1949": "national-parks-and-access-to-the-countryside-1949",
    "town and country planning act 1990": "town-and-country-planning-1990",
    "town and country planning act 1990 and the acquisition of land act 1981": "town-and-country-planning-1990-and-acquisition-of-land-1981",
    "water resources act 1991": "water-resources-1991",
    "wildlife and countryside act 1981": "wildlife-and-countryside-1981",

    # These appear in your Horizon list but are not in your ACT_ID enum:
    # "approval of commons levy rates": None,
}

# Build a Spark map literal for acts
act_map_expr = F.create_map([F.lit(x) for kv in ACT_MAP.items() for x in kv])

df_mapped = df_mapped.withColumn(
    "actId",
    F.when(F.col("act_norm").isNull() | (F.col("act_norm") == "") | (F.col("act_norm") == "n/a"), F.lit(None))
     .otherwise(act_map_expr.getItem(F.col("act_norm")))
)

# ---- SECTION mapping (string -> SECTION_ID) ----
# For ROW, sections you commonly see: 26 / 118 / 119 / 118A / 118B / 119A / 119B / 53 / 54 / 78 / Schedule 14
# NOTE: Horizon sometimes has "Schedule 14" or "SCHEDULE 14" etc.
df_mapped = df_mapped.withColumn(
    "sectionId",
    F.when(F.col("section_norm").isNull() | (F.col("section_norm") == "") | (F.col("section_norm") == "n/a"), F.lit(None))

    # numeric sections
    .when(F.col("section_norm").rlike(r"(^|\b)16(\b|$)"), F.lit("16"))
    .when(F.col("section_norm").rlike(r"(^|\b)23(\b|$)"), F.lit("23"))
    .when(F.col("section_norm").rlike(r"(^|\b)26(\b|$)"), F.lit("26"))
    .when(F.col("section_norm").rlike(r"(^|\b)38(\b|$)"), F.lit("38"))
    .when(F.col("section_norm").rlike(r"(^|\b)53(\b|$)"), F.lit("53"))
    .when(F.col("section_norm").rlike(r"(^|\b)54(\b|$)"), F.lit("54"))
    .when(F.col("section_norm").rlike(r"(^|\b)78(\b|$)"), F.lit("78"))
    .when(F.col("section_norm").rlike(r"(^|\b)118(\b|$)"), F.lit("118"))
    .when(F.col("section_norm").rlike(r"(^|\b)118a(\b|$)"), F.lit("118a"))
    .when(F.col("section_norm").rlike(r"(^|\b)118b(\b|$)"), F.lit("118b"))
    .when(F.col("section_norm").rlike(r"(^|\b)119(\b|$)"), F.lit("119"))
    .when(F.col("section_norm").rlike(r"(^|\b)119a(\b|$)"), F.lit("119a"))
    .when(F.col("section_norm").rlike(r"(^|\b)119b(\b|$)"), F.lit("119b"))

    # Articles
    .when(F.col("section_norm").rlike(r"\barticle 12\b"), F.lit("article-12"))
    .when(F.col("section_norm").rlike(r"\barticle 17\b"), F.lit("article-17"))

    # Parts / schedules
    .when(F.col("section_norm").rlike(r"\bpart 1\b.*\bschedule 6\b"), F.lit("part-1-schedule-6"))

    # Schedule 14 (ROW) -> choose D as the default when the text just says "Schedule 14"
    # If you later find a reliable way to distinguish A vs D, we can split it.
    .when(F.col("section_norm").rlike(r"\bschedule 14\b"), F.lit("schedule-14-d"))

    # Otherwise unmapped
    .otherwise(F.lit(None))
)

display(df_mapped)

row_sample = (
    df_mapped
    .withColumn("caseNodeId_str", F.col("caseNodeId").cast("string"))
    .filter(F.col("caseNodeId_str").isin(case_ids))
    .orderBy("caseNodeId_str")
    .drop("caseNodeId_str")
)

display(row_sample) 

row_sample.write \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .option("header", True) \
    .option("sep", "|") \
    .option("quote", "\"") \
    .option("escape", "\"") \
    .csv(f"{mpesc_base_path}/MPESC-TRANSFORM/case-merged-sample-row")
